opening_sequence.title: Welcome to the City of Lights, Governor
opening_sequence.intro: The economic engine of Pakistan is sputtering. The water mafia controls the tankers, the monsoon threatens to drown the streets, and political factions are armed.

advisors.name: Tariq Mahmood
advisors.hidden_agenda: Funneling provincial funds to his party's specific constituency.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Auditing the municipal tax collection apparatus.

briefing: We contribute 60% of the national revenue and get peanuts back.
The industrialists in SITE area are threatening to strike.
Inflation is killing the middle class.
disagreement: This will dry up our funding.
dialogue.betrayal: You cut off my people. Now I cut off you.
roast: Your budget has more holes than our roads.
Even the beggars manage money better than you.
scandal_reaction: Deny everything. Blame the federal government.

advisors.name: Brigadier (Retd) Arshad Khan
advisors.hidden_agenda: Expanding Ranger authority to take over civilian policing.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Reducing the Rangers' operational budget to fund local police.

briefing: Street crime in DHA is up 15%.
We need authorization for a targeted operation in Lyari.
The civilian police are useless, let my men handle it.
agreement: Order is the only currency that matters.
disagreement: You are letting the city burn.
dialogue.betrayal: If you won't lead, the uniform will. You are irrelevant now.
roast: You have the backbone of a jellyfish.

advisors.name: Zahra Ali
advisors.hidden_agenda: Dismantling the tanker mafia to provide piped water to all slums.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Granting formal municipal contracts to the private water tanker unions.

briefing: Half the city doesn't have piped water.
The monsoon drains are clogged with garbage again.
Land grabbers are encroaching on the Malir riverbed.
agreement: A step towards a liveable city.
disagreement: We are building our own ruin.
dialogue.betrayal: You sold our water to thugs. I'm taking this to the Supreme Court.
roast: Your vision for the city is a concrete nightmare.
You couldn't plan a picnic, let alone a metropolis.
scandal_reaction: The rot starts at the top.

advisors.name: Bilal Ahmed
advisors.hidden_agenda: Delaying the Green Line BRT to protect the private bus operators' cartel.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Fully funding and launching the BRT rapid transit system.

briefing: The Qingqi rickshaws are protesting the ban.
Traffic on Shahrah-e-Faisal is a dead halt.
The transport union demands higher fares.
dialogue.betrayal: You ruined the transport cartel. You won't survive the strike.
roast: You're driving us off a cliff.
scandal_reaction: I suggest you leave town for a few days.

advisors.name: Mufti Usman
advisors.hidden_agenda: Securing prime municipal land for a mega-seminary.
advisors.betrayal_trigger: Bulldozing an illegally constructed mosque on public land.

briefing: The ulema have concerns about the new curriculum.
Morality in the city is declining.
disagreement: You are straying from the right path.
The faithful will not forget this.
dialogue.betrayal: You have declared war on the faith. Expect a million on the streets.
roast: Even prayers cannot fix your incompetence.
scandal_reaction: This is a punishment for our sins.

decisions.title: The Water Crisis
decisions.body: Piped water is non-existent in many areas. The private 'tanker mafia' controls the supply and is price-gouging residents during a heatwave.

options.label: Subsidize the tankers to lower prices temporarily.

consequences.public_reaction: At least we have water today.

options.label: Deploy the Rangers to seize the hydrants from the mafia.

consequences.public_reaction: Finally taking action against these thugs!

options.label: Grant the mafia an official monopoly in exchange for a massive kickback to the city budget.

consequences.public_reaction: The government is just the biggest mafia of them all.

decisions.title: Clearing the Nullahs
decisions.body: Monsoon season is approaching. The main stormwater drains (nullahs) are clogged with trash and illegal settlements.

options.label: Hire temporary workers for a superficial cleanup.

consequences.public_reaction: This won't save us when the rain comes.

options.label: Evict the illegal settlements and widen the drains.

consequences.public_reaction: Cruel to the poor, but necessary for the city.

options.label: Ignore the drains and distribute cheap life jackets.

decisions.title: Mobile Snatching Epidemic
decisions.body: Armed robberies and phone snatchings have spiked drastically. Citizens are demanding action.

options.label: Increase police patrols in affluent areas.

consequences.public_reaction: Only protecting the rich, as usual.

options.label: Authorize a harsh Ranger crackdown across all districts.

consequences.public_reaction: Finally, I can take my phone out in public.

options.label: Ban motorcycles with pillion riders to stop drive-by snatchings.

consequences.public_reaction: How am I supposed to take my child to school?!

decisions.title: Power Grid Collapse
decisions.body: Unannounced power outages are lasting up to 14 hours a day in the industrial zones. Factories are shutting down.

options.label: Divert power from residential areas to keep factories running.

consequences.public_reaction: We are sweating in the dark while the elite make money.

options.label: Fine the private power utility massively for breach of contract.
Encourage citizens to steal electricity from the grid.

decisions.title: The Ghost Train
decisions.body: Funds for the Karachi Circular Railway revival have mysteriously vanished. The project is stalled again.

options.label: Delay the project indefinitely.
Launch a corruption inquiry and seek international loans.

consequences.public_reaction: We will have modern transport in this decade!

options.label: Pave over the railway tracks to create toll roads.

consequences.public_reaction: Destroying heritage for a quick buck.

crises.name: Urban Flooding Catastrophe
crises.trigger: Record-breaking monsoon rains have submerged half the city.
crises.description: The drainage system has completely failed. DHA is underwater, and the slums are washing away. Power is out across the metropolis. 40 people are dead and citizens are rioting outside the utility company offices.

options.label: Beg the military to handle the rescue and relief efforts.
Liquidate municipal assets to pay for massive emergency pumps and aid.
Blame the previous administration and focus solely on protecting government zones.

crises.name: Targeted Killing Ignites City
crises.trigger: A prominent political leader has been gunned down in Nazimabad.
crises.description: Party loyalists have paralyzed the city. Buses are being burned, businesses are forced shut, and armed men are patrolling the streets. The police are vastly outgunned.

options.label: Declare a curfew and shut down cellular networks.
Arrest top opposition leaders accused of orchestrating the hit.
Pay massive 'compensation' to the victim's party to buy peace.

crises.name: IMF Demands Cutbacks
crises.trigger: The federal government has agreed to IMF terms, and municipal subsidies must be slashed instantly.
crises.description: Bread prices have doubled overnight. Transit fares are up 50%. The working class is threatening a general strike that will shut down the ports.

options.label: Comply with the cuts and brace for riots.
Defy the federal government and maintain local subsidies.
Print unregulated local bonds to cover the shortfall.

crises.name: Supreme Court Anti-Encroachment Drive
crises.trigger: The highest court mandates the immediate demolition of all illegal structures in the city.
crises.description: This means bulldozing historic markets, thousands of homes, and businesses. If you refuse, you face contempt of court and jail. If you proceed, a million people lose their livelihoods.

options.label: Send in the bulldozers and enforce the court order.
Refuse the order and resign in protest, triggering early elections.
Bribe the surveyors to declare the illegal structures 'historic'.

scandals.title: Thousands of 'Ghost' Employees Discovered
scandals.description: Audits reveal that 30% of the municipal workforce doesn't actually exist. You've been paying salaries to dead people for years.

scandals.title: Hospital Waste Dumped in Lyari River
scandals.description: Footage shows government trucks illegally dumping toxic medical waste into the riverbed to save money.

scandals.title: Governor Linked to Major Land Grabbing Syndicate
scandals.description: Leaked audio implicates you in a scheme violently evicting poor residents to hand land to a private developer.

positive: A rare moment of sanity in this city.
Let's see if it lasts past tomorrow.
neutral: We survive despite the government, not because of it.
Let's see if the mafia lets this happen.
I'll believe it when I see it.
negative: Bera gharq kar diya is shehar ka.
Where is our tax money going?!
Looters and thieves, all of them.
media: Geo News: Public outrage over recent municipal decision.
Dawn: A step forward or a step back for Karachi?
ARY News: Opposition leaders demand accountability.
Samaa: Traffic remains jammed across the metropolis.
Express Tribune: Economic fallout expected from city hall's latest move.
Capital TV: Is the Governor losing grip on the city?
politician: We will protest this injustice on the streets!
A necessary evil for long-term stability.
This is an attack on the common man.
We stand with the people against this tyranny.
activist: We need trees, not more concrete.
End the elite capture of this city.
Our lives matter more than your profits.
street: Phone chupa lo, area theek nahi hai.
Tanker wala is asking for 5000 rupees today!
Kunda laga lo, koi faida nahi inko pay karne ka.
Traffic on Shahrah-e-Faisal is mental today.
crisis: We are drowning and no one cares.
Stay indoors, it's not safe outside.
scandal: They are all corrupt to the core.
Nothing surprises me anymore in this country.
How much did they make from this?

scandal_reactions.major: Bring them to the streets and make them pay!

romance_exposure.flavour: In a deeply conservative society, the scandal is viewed as a severe moral failing, leading to immediate demands for resignation from religious clerics and the public alike.
